from source import filter_expensive_items

def test_filter_expensive_items_string_price():
    """
    Test that filter_expensive_items handles string prices without crashing
    and correctly compares them numerically.
    """
    items = [
        {'name': 'A', 'price': 100},
        {'name': 'B', 'price': '200'}   # string price triggers the bug
    ]
    threshold = 150

    # This call should succeed on fixed code; on buggy code it raises TypeError.
    result = filter_expensive_items(items, threshold)

    # Assert correct filtering: only item B (price "200" > 150) should be returned.
    expected_names = ['B']
    assert len(result) == 1, f"Expected 1 item, got {len(result)}"
    assert result[0]['name'] == 'B', f"Expected item B, got {result[0]['name']}"
